conversation.text: Daily, fetch posts from an API, filter some out, and post the rest to a Slack channel.
Which API endpoint, what's the filter, and which Slack channel?

GET https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts. Drop any post whose title contains the word 'qui'. Post to #api-digest as a single summary message that says how many posts remain and lists their titles.
What should the Slack message look like?
It should say how many posts remain after filtering and list each remaining post's title.
Got it. Anything else before I build it?
One more thing — if every post gets filtered out, I still want the Slack message posted that day saying 0 posts remain. Don't skip the message just because nothing is left.

executionScenarios.name: happy-path
executionScenarios.description: API returns posts, some contain 'qui' and get filtered, summary posted to Slack
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The HTTP Request node returns JSON objects with fields: id, userId, title, body. Some of them should have 'qui' in the title (e.g. 'qui est esse', 'nesciunt quid non qui'). Others should NOT contain 'qui'. The Slack node returns a success response.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. Posts containing 'qui' in the title are filtered out. The Slack message is posted to #api-digest and includes how many posts remain plus the titles of the remaining posts. None of the titles in the Slack message should contain the word 'qui'.

executionScenarios.name: all-filtered
executionScenarios.description: Every post contains 'qui' in the title and all are filtered out
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The HTTP Request node returns 3 posts, all with 'qui' in their titles. The Slack node returns a success response.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: All posts are filtered out and a Slack message is still posted to #api-digest stating that 0 posts remain. The workflow does not crash and does not send an empty or malformed message.
